questions.question: A cylindrical water tank has radius 4 m and height 10 m. A cone with the same base radius is carved out from the top, with height 6 m. What is the volume of the remaining solid in cubic meters?
questions.explanation: Volume of cylinder = πr²h = π(4²)(10) = 160π. Volume of cone = (1/3)πr²h = (1/3)π(4²)(6) = 32π. Remaining volume = 160π − 32π = 128π = 496π/3.88... Recalculate: 160π − 32π = 128π cubic meters.

questions.question: A sphere has surface area 144π square units. Find its volume.
questions.explanation: Surface area = 4πr² = 144π, so r² = 36, thus r = 6. Volume = (4/3)πr³ = (4/3)π(6³) = (4/3)π(216) = 288π cubic units.

questions.question: A rectangular prism has dimensions 5 cm × 12 cm × 13 cm. A cylindrical hole of radius 2 cm is drilled completely through the prism along its longest dimension (13 cm). What is the remaining volume in cubic centimeters?
questions.explanation: Original prism volume = 5 × 12 × 13 = 780 cm³. Cylindrical hole volume = πr²h = π(2²)(13) = 52π cm³. Remaining = 780 − 52π cm³.

questions.question: Two similar cones have radii in the ratio 2:3. If the smaller cone has volume 16π cubic units, what is the volume of the larger cone?
questions.explanation: For similar solids, volume scales as the cube of linear dimensions. If radii are in ratio 2:3, volumes are in ratio 2³:3³ = 8:27. So 16π : V = 8 : 27 gives V = 16π × 27/8 = 54π cubic units.

questions.question: A cone has slant height 13 cm and base radius 5 cm. What is its volume in cubic centimeters?
questions.explanation: Use Pythagorean theorem to find height: h² + 5² = 13², so h² = 169 − 25 = 144, thus h = 12 cm. Volume = (1/3)πr²h = (1/3)π(5²)(12) = 100π cm³.

questions.question: A sphere is inscribed in a cube with side length 8. What percent of the cube's volume is NOT occupied by the sphere? Round to the nearest whole percent.
questions.explanation: Cube volume = 8³ = 512. Inscribed sphere has diameter 8, so radius = 4. Sphere volume = (4/3)π(4³) = (256π/3) ≈ 268.1. Unoccupied = 512 − 268.1 ≈ 243.9. Percentage = (243.9/512) × 100 ≈ 48%.
